name: Lore motion system
source: Codified from docs/brand/MOTION_SYSTEM.md (Lore brand-authoritative motion doc). v0.2.0 adds the liveness primitives harvested from the ROLR prediction-market product.

purpose_gate.rule: Every motion element must satisfy at least one of: orientation, proof, delight, liveness. If none — remove it.

purposes.orientation: Where am I in the story? (e.g. Snap-Step scroll locks, slide-in screen transitions)
purposes.proof: Show product behavior (e.g. chart line drawing in, odds bar filling, count-up)
purposes.delight: Subtle polish (e.g. hero card float, settlement confetti)
purposes.liveness: Is this surface live right now? Real-time presence, streaming price, an active pot. Added for ROLR — a prediction market's whole value is that it is happening now. Use liveness motion only where the data is genuinely real-time; faking it is the anti-pattern.

float.description: Subtle vertical drift or parallax on hero objects. Ambient.

snap_step.description: Scroll locks briefly at key beats. One snap = one idea.

reveal.description: Fade in + slight rise (8-16px) + optional blur-to-sharp.

trace.description: Chart lines draw in; numbers count up; skeleton -> real data. Provides credibility.

magnet.description: Subtle magnetic pull on hover/touch; firm press feedback. Interaction confidence.

glow_pulse.description: Soft accent-color glow on active cards, selected tabs, primary CTA. Never continuous everywhere.

live_pulse.description: Opacity pulse (1 -> 0.25 -> 1) on a small presence dot. The universal 'this is live right now' indicator: live demo headers, 'N playing now', latency chips, in-play scores. Harvested from ROLR `livedot`/`blink`.
live_pulse.usage: Pair with --live (presence) or a market/status color. The dot is 7-9px; never animate the label text, only the dot.

win_glow.description: Tight accent ring + glow pulse on a winning / live-active cell (Pool square, Bingo cell, resolving-soon leader). Harder and faster than glow_pulse. Harvested from ROLR `winglow`.
win_glow.usage: Exactly one win-glow per board at a time — it marks the single currently-winning cell. When the winning cell moves, move the glow.

stream.description: Real-time data streaming: a sparkline extends one point at a time and live numbers count toward their new value. JS-driven (setInterval ~1s), not a CSS loop. Harvested from the ROLR Tick price feed + pot/player counters.
stream.implementation: On each tick, append a point to the series (shift to keep a fixed window) and re-render the polyline; for scalar values, ease the displayed number toward the target. Recolor the line by --up/--down vs the previous value.
stream.usage: Cap at 2-3 streaming series per viewport. Always tabular slashed-zero so digits don't jitter width. Respect prefers-reduced-motion: show the latest value statically, no count animation.

pop.description: Scale overshoot (0.7 -> 1.06 -> 1) on a number/badge that just changed or a result that just landed. Harvested from ROLR `popin`.
pop.usage: One-shot, on value change or mount. The only place ROLR allows spring/overshoot. Don't loop it.

feed_in.description: Activity-feed row entry: rise 10px + fade. New rows enter at the top of a live feed (a bet placed, a square claimed, a member joined). Harvested from ROLR `feedin`.
feed_in.usage: Stagger newest-first; cap stagger at 120ms. Only animate genuinely new rows, never the whole list on re-render.

slide_in.description: Screen/overlay transition: slide in from the right (~26px) + fade. Game overlays, settlement screens, step-to-step flows on the phone surface. Harvested from ROLR `slidein`.

bar_fill.description: Odds / progress bar grows from scaleX(0) to scaleX(1) on mount. Gives the probability split a sense of being measured. Harvested from ROLR `barfill`.
bar_fill.usage: transform-origin:left. Animate on first paint only; subsequent odds changes should tween width, not re-fill.

breathe.description: Slow scale+opacity pulse (1 -> 1.06) on a single live hero number (a probability that is updating live, an in-play percentage). Heavier sibling of float, for one focal number. Harvested from ROLR `breathe`.
breathe.usage: Maximum one per viewport, reserved for the single most-live number on screen.

confetti.description: Settlement celebration: particles fall + rotate from above. One-shot on a win/settlement screen only. Harvested from ROLR `confettiFall`.
confetti.usage: Settlement / win moments only. Never ambient, never on a dashboard. 16-24 particles, randomized left/size/duration/delay, theme accent + up/down colors.

ambient.usage: live_pulse, win_glow, breathe — organic, breathing. ROLR signature; inverse of revenant's mechanical linear.

linear.usage: Never use as primary easing (revenant ambient is the deliberate exception)

budget_per_page.live_pulse: unlimited (presence dots are cheap and meaningful)
reduction_priority: Extra Glow Pulse -> limit to primary CTA only

viewport_rules.reduced_motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Disable Float, Glow Pulse, Trace, Live Pulse, Win Glow, Breathe, Confetti. Keep Reveal/Feed-in/Slide-in as instant opacity. Stream shows latest value with no count animation.

anti_patterns: Bounce easing (pop's overshoot is the single sanctioned exception)
Multiple competing focal animations per section
Animation re-trigger on scroll-up (use viewport.once: true)
Faking liveness: live_pulse / stream / breathe on data that is not actually real-time
More than one win-glow per board
Confetti anywhere but a settlement/win moment

css_var_contract.note: Themes override these vars in agent/themes/<theme>/colors_and_type.css. Consumers read them via var() in CSS or via the motion.css helpers below. --motion-duration-live, --motion-duration-pop and --motion-ease-pop are added in v0.2.0 for the liveness primitives.
